methodology.summary: Measures duplicate detection performance on manually labeled semantic claim pairs.

pairs.reason: Both state that greplica install accepts --hooks enabled|disabled and --auto-memory enabled|disabled with defaults enabled.
Both state that greplica install accepts --hooks enabled|disabled and --auto-memory enabled|disabled with defaults enabled.
Both state that greplica install now parses/accepts --hooks and --auto-memory as enabled/disabled values.
Both state that Install now accepts --hooks enabled|disabled and --auto-memory enabled|disabled flags.
Both state that no-hooks onboarding does not modify AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and prints a reusable guidance snippet.
Both state that in No hooks onboarding, Greplica must not edit AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and should print the hook guidance snippet.
Both state that with hooks disabled, onboarding no longer edits AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and prints the shared Greplica hook guidance snippet.
Both state that hook guidance and background memory updates are separate; guidance can still emit but worker starts only when autoMemoryUpdates is true.
Both state that Session config includes autoMemoryUpdates with default true, install writes this flag, forces false when hooks disabled.
Both state that install writes session.autoMemoryUpdates into config and forces it false when hooks are not installed.
Both state that onboarding asks exactly two questions before install: guidance mode and prior-session backfill.
Both state that Greplica install uses a two-question onboarding questionnaire for hook/auto-memory choices.
Both state that onboarding is agent-led before install, asks for hook/guidance choice and prior-session backfill.
Both state that factory-droid installer honors --hooks disabled by installing skills but skipping hook config writes.
Both state that Factory-Droid installer respects --hooks disabled by preventing hook config writes.
Both state that SWE-chat plan eval cases should be data-only with case.json, prompt.md, judge.md, memory-seeds.
Both state that a generic SWE-chat plan case is now data-only with case.json, prompt.md, judge.md, memory-seeds.
Both state that SWE-chat plan cases should not prescribe the exact Greplica retrieval query or store memory.retrieval_query.
Both state that the Greplica arm no longer stores/hard-codes a retrieval_query; agent chooses its own query.
Both state that SWE-chat plan Greplica arm no longer hard-codes a retrieval query; agent chooses its own.
Both state that the memory builder materializes repo, bootstraps, processes prior_sessions, uses raw tool/edit records only for diff, passes filtered transcript to agent.
Both state the same memory builder process: materialize repo, bootstrap, process prior sessions, use raw records only for diff.
Both state that the builder passes only filtered human/assistant messages to the agent; raw tool-call JSONL is used only for reconstructing diffs.
Both state that the memory builder passes filtered Markdown transcripts to update agents and uses raw edit records only for diff reconstruction.
Both state that memory generation is case-driven, projects JSONL into filtered Markdown, reconstructs edits from toolUseResult for diff.
Both describe the same install flag behavior with different wording: one says 'accepts --hooks enabled|disabled' and the other says 'accepts --hooks enabled|disabled flags (default true)'.
Both say no-hooks onboarding does not edit AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and prints a reusable guidance snippet, with different phrasing.
Both describe autoMemoryUpdates default true and install forcing it false when hooks are unavailable, with different wording.
Both describe the two-question onboarding flow with different wording: one says 'asks exactly two questions' and the other says 'prompts for hook/auto-memory choices'.
Both state that factory-droid installer honors --hooks disabled by installing skills but skipping hook config writes, with different wording.

Both state that the Greplica arm no longer hard-codes a retrieval query and the agent chooses its own, with different wording.
Both describe the same memory builder process: materialize repo, bootstrap, process prior sessions, use raw records only for diff, pass filtered transcript.
Both state that the builder passes only filtered human/assistant messages to the agent and uses raw tool-call JSONL only for diff reconstruction.
Both describe the case-driven memory build process with filtered transcripts and edit reconstruction, with different wording.
